q: The branch of physics and technology concerned with the design of circuits using transistors, integrated circuits and microchips is known as electronics.
Active electrical components include vacuum tubes, transistors, diodes, ICs, TRIACs, SCRs, and LEDs.
Passive electrical components are capable of operating without an external power source.
Resistors, capacitors, inductors, and transformers are classified as active electrical components.
Zener diode is a heavily doped semiconductor (silicon or germanium) pn junction diode.
A zener diode makes the current flow in the backward direction when reverse biased.
A zener diode has a turn-on voltage of between $0.3$ and $0.7 V$ in the forward-bias mode.
The effect known as the zener effect occurs when the reverse voltage reaches the breakdown voltage and large current starts flowing.
A normal diode allows current to flow in both directions.
Avalanche breakdown occurs at junctions which being lightly doped have wide depletion layers.
Zener breakdown occurs at junctions which being heavily doped have narrow depletion layers.
For normal operation of a zener diode in breakdown region, the current through the diode should be limited by an external circuit.
The voltage drop across the zener diode always remains constant irrespective of the applied voltage.
A LED is a semiconductor light source that emits light when current flows through it.
A light emitting diode (LED) is known to be one of the best optoelectronic devices.
The visible lights that an LED emits are usually orange, red, yellow, or green.
The commonly used invisible light for LED is ultraviolet light.
LEDs are made from exotic semiconductor compounds such as Gallium Arsenide (GaAs).
In LED the energy is radiated in the form of light.
LEDs directly produce white light.
A blue LED with a phosphor coating can convert blue light to white light by a process called fluorescence.
LEDs are typically used as ON/OFF indicator lights in electrical appliances.
A current limiting resistor should be connected in series with LED in the circuit to protect the LED.
LEDs have more power consumption, exceeding $150 mW$.
LEDs have fast action and no warmup time, with a response time of about $10 ns$.
The LED device is known to have a much wider bandwidth compared to the laser.
A photodiode is a semiconductor device that converts light into electrical current.
Photodiodes work in forward biased mode.
When the photodiode is illuminated with light of energy $h \nu$ greater than the energy gap $E_g$ of the semiconductor, then electron-hole pairs are generated.
The magnitude of the photocurrent depends on the intensity of incident light.
A small amount of current, called dark current, is produced when no light is falling on the photodiode.
Silicon is most often used to fabricate photodiodes, though germanium and gallium arsenide can also be used.
A light dependent resistor (LDR) is a light controlled variable resistor.
LDR consists of a disc of semiconductor material on its surface with two electrodes.
In the dark or in dim light, the material of the LDR disc has a relatively large number of free electrons in it.
The dark resistance of an LDR is quite high, around $M \Omega$.
As light level increases, the LDR resistance decreases to a few hundred $\Omega$.
When LDR is exposed to light, it takes a few seconds to lower its resistance.
A thermistor is a temperature sensor that exhibits a large change in resistance proportional to a change in temperature.
Negative Temperature Coefficient (NTC) thermistor resistance decreases as the temperature increases.
Positive Temperature Coefficient (PTC) thermistor resistance increases as the temperature increases.
Liquid crystals are a state of matter which has properties between those of conventional liquids and those of solid crystals.
Liquid crystals do not emit light directly, instead using a backlight or reflector.
LCDs allowed displays to be much thinner than cathode ray tube (CRT) technology.
LCDs consume much less power than LEDs and gas-plasma displays.
A polarizer is an optical device that can convert an unpolarized light wave into a polarized light wave by blocking all other vibrations.
Digital electronics is a field of electronics involving the study of digital signals and the engineering of devices that use or produce them.
An advantage of digital circuits is that signals represented digitally can be transmitted without degradation caused by noise.
An IC (integrated circuit) is a semiconductor wafer on which thousands or millions of tiny resistors, capacitors and transistors are fabricated.
Linear ICs have continuously variable output that depends on the input signal level.
Digital ICs operate over a continuous range of signal amplitudes.
Logic 0 represents the LOW state and Logic 1 represents the HIGH state.
The fundamental building blocks of digital ICs are logic gates.
The AND gate with inverted output is called a NAND gate.
The OR gate with inverted output is called a NOR gate.
In an XOR gate, the output is HIGH if one, and only one, of the inputs is HIGH.
XOR gate produces a 0 when both inputs match.
XNOR gate is an XOR gate whose output is inverted.
Boolean algebra is a branch of mathematics where variables can have only two possible values: false and true.
Boolean algebra is used to analyze and simplify digital (logic) circuits.
The commutative, associative and distributive laws for addition and multiplication in Boolean algebra are different from ordinary algebra.
The commutative law of addition is expressed as $A + B = B + A$.
The commutative law of multiplication is expressed as $A \cdot B = B \cdot A$.
The associative law of addition is expressed as $A + (B + C) = (A + B) + C$.
The distributive law is expressed as $A(B + C) = AB + AC$.
According to basic rules of Boolean algebra, $A + 1 = 1$.
According to basic rules of Boolean algebra, $A \cdot 0 = 0$.
According to basic rules of Boolean algebra, $A + A = A$.
The rule for double inversion is $\overline{\overline{A}} = A$.
De Morgan's first theorem states that the complement of two or more ANDed variables is equivalent to the OR of the complements of the individual variables.
The formula for De Morgan's first theorem for two variables is $\overline{A \cdot B} = \overline{A} + \overline{B}$.
De Morgan's second theorem states that the complement of two or more ORed variables is equivalent to the AND of the complements of the individual variables.
The formula for De Morgan's second theorem for two variables is $\overline{A + B} = \overline{A} \cdot \overline{B}$.
Logic gates can be used as processors in electronic control systems.
In a fire alarm system, the fire alarm will turn on when either smoke or heat is detected.
In a fire alarm system, the fire extinguisher will operate if both smoke and heat are detected.
In a street lighting system, the OR gate gives a 1 output when either or both of its inputs are 1.
An electronic communication system is composed of the transmission, reception, and processing of information between two or more locations.
The basic components of an electronic communication system are the transmitter, communication channel, receiver and noise.
Information source is a device that generates information to be transmitted.
Transmitter processes the message signal into an appropriate form for transmission and subsequent reception.
Channel is the medium that connects transmitter and receiver to convey the information.
Noise is an unwanted signal that disturbs, interferes and affects the transmitted signal.
Noise cannot be prevented but can be minimized.
Digital signals are more immune to noise than analog signals.
Receiver reconstructs the transmitted signal and delivers it to the destination called user of information.
In point-to-point mode, communication takes place over a link between a single transmitter and a receiver.
Radio and television are examples of point-to-point communication.
In the broadcast mode, there are a large number of receivers corresponding to a single transmitter.
Analog communication uses an analog signal which varies in amplitude, phase, or some other property with time.
Data can be broken into packets as discrete messages in analog communication.
In line communication, there is a physical connection between source and destination.
A transmitter encodes a message onto electromagnetic waves through modulation.
A transducer is a device for converting a non-electrical input into an electrical signal (or) vice versa.
The process of converting energy from one form to another is known as transduction.
Loudspeakers, microphones, and LEDs are common examples of transducers.
The process of changing some characteristics of a carrier wave in accordance with the intensity of the signal is known as modulation.
Modulation permits the transmission to occur at high frequency while simultaneously allowing the carrying of the audio signal.
There are three basic types of modulation: Amplitude modulation (AM), Frequency modulation (FM), and Phase modulation (PM).
In amplitude modulation, only the amplitude of the carrier wave is changed in accordance with the intensity of the signal.
In amplitude modulation, the frequency of the modulated wave remains the same.
In frequency modulation, only the frequency of the carrier wave is changed in accordance with the signal.
In frequency modulation, the amplitude of the modulated wave remains the same.
FM is widely used for broadcasting music and speech, two-way radio systems, and magnetic tape-recording systems.
Phase modulation encodes information as variations in the instantaneous phase of a carrier wave.
Unlike FM, phase modulation is widely used for transmitting radio waves.
The process of recovering the audio signal from the modulated wave is known as demodulation or detection.
A simple radio receiver comprises antenna, tuner, rf amplifier, diode detector, af amplifier and speaker.
The receiving antenna picks up the radio signals which is coupled to tuning circuit through the inductor $L$.
The input signal to the diode detector contains an rf component and an af component.
During each carrier cycle, the diode turns on briefly and charges the capacitor in the detection circuit to the peak voltage.
The time constant $RC$ should be greater than the period of the carrier wave for the capacitor to discharge slightly between cycles.
